message:
bd-2k90, bd-2cyz: Non-TTY smoke verification tests

- Add 5 piped output verification tests (bd-2k90):
  - test_piped_all_scenes_complete: verifies each scene completes when piped
  - test_piped_output_is_readable_text: checks for no control chars
  - test_piped_no_blocking_pager: verifies no pager blocking
  - test_piped_per_scene_output_bounded: guards against runaway output
  - test_piped_quick_mode_is_fast: performance gate for CI
- Close bd-2cyz: hero scene snapshot already created in bd-cisk
- Update scene list test to include table and traceback scenes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-26gp","title":"demo_showcase: feature strategy (Cargo flags)","description":"Decide how demo_showcase relates to crate features (recommend `--features full,tracing` vs add a `showcase` alias vs `[[bin]].required-features`).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealCave","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:23.968961955Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:46:28.082021959Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","features"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26gp","depends_on_id":"bd-2b0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:46.057307163Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-26gp","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:23.984489073Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":64,"issue_id":"bd-26gp","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Decision points

We need a clear story for “how to run the full demo”:

Option A (simplest):
- Document `--features full,tracing` and keep demo code feature-gated.

Option B (nice UX):
- Add a new feature alias, e.g. `showcase = ["full", "tracing"]`.
- Document `--features showcase`.

Option C (strict):
- Declare `[[bin]]` in Cargo.toml and set `required-features = ["full", "tracing"]` so the binary only builds when fully enabled.
- Risk: `cargo run --bin demo_showcase` fails without extra flags (less friendly).

## Acceptance criteria

- New users can run the “best” demo with one obvious command.
- Default `cargo run --bin demo_showcase` should still do something reasonable (unless we explicitly choose strict required-features).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2b0s","title":"demo_showcase: narrative + scene storyboard","description":"Choose the demo storyline, name, copy, and the ordered scene list (full run + single-scene names).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:12.806761210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:10.217861682Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:10.217788947Z","close_reason":"Completed (spec in README demo_showcase section)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2b0s","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:12.821701513Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":16,"issue_id":"bd-2b0s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Output deliverable

Produce a concrete storyboard so implementation can follow without ambiguity.

### Recommended scene list (example)

1. `hero` – branding + capabilities + palette preview
2. `dashboard` – live layout with services/pipeline/logs
3. `deep_dive_markdown` – release notes / runbook
4. `deep_dive_syntax` – config/code snippet
5. `deep_dive_json` – API payloads
6. `debug_tools` – pretty/inspect + traceback + logging/tracing
7. `export` – HTML/SVG bundle
8. `outro` – summary table/tree + next steps

### Naming

Pick a fictional product name and keep it consistent in copy, e.g. “Nebula Deploy”.

## Acceptance criteria

- Each scene has a 1–2 sentence purpose description.
- Each scene lists which rich_rust features it must exercise.
- Single-scene mode (`--scene <name>`) uses these names verbatim.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2cjt","title":"demo_showcase: interactive start wizard","description":"Add an optional interactive start wizard using Prompt (scenario/speed/export choices).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:20.004600090Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:49.118079718Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","prompt"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","depends_on_id":"bd-12nv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:46.637019230Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:49.118050083Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","depends_on_id":"bd-1o8x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:44.192163526Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","depends_on_id":"bd-3rhg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:20.017001830Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":56,"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Wizard questions (suggested)

- Run mode: full demo vs specific scene
- Speed: normal vs quick
- Export: yes/no

Keep it short and skippable (defaults).

## Acceptance criteria

- Only runs when interactive and not disabled.
- Falls back to defaults otherwise.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:31Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2fl5","title":"demo_showcase: visual design system (theme/palette)","description":"Define a cohesive theme/palette and named styles that make the demo look like a real product.","notes":"Completed via child beads: bd-1by8 (theme/palette definitions in theme.rs) and bd-313x (typography patterns in typography.rs). Named styles include brand.*, status.*, section.*, panel.*, table.*, log.* prefixes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:17.976813041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:43:16.629814050Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:43:16.629672817Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fl5","depends_on_id":"bd-2b0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:24.788358650Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2fl5","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:18.006687094Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"bd-2fl5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

Make the demo visually consistent and “brandable” using the Theme system and rich_rust style primitives. This is where we turn the output from “works” into “wow”.

## Design principles

- Use a small, consistent palette: primary/accent/success/warn/error + neutrals.
- Prefer named styles via `Theme` so users can copy patterns (`status.ok`, `status.warn`, `panel.title`, etc.).
- Keep readability on low-color terminals: ensure acceptable downgrades to 16/256 colors.
- Use whitespace, rules, and alignment to create hierarchy (like a UI, not a log dump).

## Deliverables

- A `Theme::from_style_definitions(...)` set used by the demo’s Console.
- Named styles for:
  - headers / hero
  - section rules
  - table header + key columns
  - status badges (OK/WARN/ERR)
  - log levels
  - “call to action” links
- Optional: a small helper to render “badges” like `[bold white on green] OK [/ ]`.

## Acceptance criteria

- The demo uses named styles in *most* scenes (not just ad-hoc `Style::parse`).
- The demo still looks coherent when forced to `ColorSystem::Standard`.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2hba","title":"demo_showcase: UI helper primitives","description":"Implement small UI helpers (badges, section headers, key/value rows) using Text/Style.","notes":"Added kv_row(), kv_row_styled(), badge() helpers to typography.rs. Combined with existing status_badge(), status_text(), section_header() from bd-313x, all requested UI primitives are now available.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:01.342641761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:49:16.161120916Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:49:16.160994450Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hba","depends_on_id":"bd-1by8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:23.099136270Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hba","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:01.357952838Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hba","depends_on_id":"bd-313x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:25.537534583Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":26,"issue_id":"bd-2hba","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Helpers to build

Small, reusable building blocks for the demo binary:
- `badge(text, style_name)` -> `Text` (or markup snippet)
- `kv_row(key, value)` -> renderable suitable for panels
- `section_title("...")` -> rule + aligned title

These keep the demo consistent and prevent copy/paste styling drift.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2j06","title":"demo_showcase: SVG export writer","description":"Implement SVG export writing and print the output path.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:58.528362072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:46.373008611Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","export","svg"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2j06","depends_on_id":"bd-164e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:58.544199353Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2j06","depends_on_id":"bd-86gp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:46.372982102Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":52,"issue_id":"bd-2j06","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## SVG export

Use `Console::export_svg(true)` (or configured variant) and write to `demo_showcase.svg`.

## Acceptance criteria

- SVG contains representative multi-style output (not just text).
- Demo prints a short note that SVG uses `<foreignObject>` and is best viewed in modern browsers.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:45:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2jec","title":"Conformance: enable fixture-based parity for Syntax/Markdown/JSON","description":"Strengthen Python Rich parity by expanding conformance fixtures beyond plain-text comparisons.

Scope:
- Update Python fixture generator + schema as needed so syntax/markdown/json have stable expectations.
- Normalize unstable fields (theme names, random ids, whitespace) where needed.

Acceptance:
- At least one new fixture each for Syntax/Markdown/JSON where compare_ansi=true (or clearly documented why not).
- Rust conformance test passes under: cargo test --test conformance_python --features "conformance_test,syntax,markdown,json".","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:35:16.789265580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:10.121693324Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:10.121625438Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2jec","depends_on_id":"bd-3c90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:35:16.808226745Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2k8w","title":"demo_showcase: tree showcase scene","description":"Create a tree scene (deployment plan / dependency graph) demonstrating guide styles, icons, collapsed nodes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:42:14.381218326Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:52:12.272542547Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","tree"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2k8w","depends_on_id":"bd-1by8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:52:12.272514886Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2k8w","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:52:09.873110093Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2k8w","depends_on_id":"bd-2175","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:42:14.396654668Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":39,"issue_id":"bd-2k8w","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Tree content

Use a tree to present something users actually do:
- deployment plan steps and sub-steps
- service dependency graph
- file structure for a release artifact

Demonstrate:
- guides variants
- icons/emoji
- collapsed nodes

## Acceptance criteria

- Tree integrates with theme styles (guide style + node highlight).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:42:49Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2llx","title":"Regression test: Cell::new() vs Cell::from_markup()","description":"## Problem
Cell::new() was silently NOT parsing markup - users expected Cell::new("[bold]text[/]") to work but it didn't. We added Cell::from_markup() but need regression tests to prevent this from happening again and document the difference clearly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test that explicitly shows Cell::new("[bold]text[/]") does NOT parse markup (expected behavior, documents it)
- [ ] Test that explicitly shows Cell::from_markup("[bold]text[/]") DOES parse markup
- [ ] Test the difference in output between the two methods
- [ ] Add doc comments explaining when to use each method

## Implementation Notes
- The tests should be clear about the EXPECTED difference between the two methods
- This documents API behavior and prevents confusion

## Files to modify
- src/renderables/table.rs - improve doc comments
- tests/conformance/table_tests.rs - add regression tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:27.981429151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:25:04.610863384Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:25:04.610835742Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 6 regression tests documenting Cell::new() vs Cell::from_markup() difference. Updated Cell::new() doc comments with clear examples. All tests pass, clippy and fmt clean.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2nv","title":"Targeted code review pass + file findings","description":"Perform focused code review for correctness/perf risks; file follow-up beads for any findings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T16:59:53.973055499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:21:57.601872493Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:21:57.601543252Z","close_reason":"Completed: filed findings bd-yb9 + bd-1wk","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2pe5","title":"demo_showcase: README docs","description":"Add a Demo Showcase section to README with recommended commands and what it demonstrates.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:55.945257835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:50.973620010Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pe5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:55:09.161637790Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pe5","depends_on_id":"bd-26gp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:50.973585556Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pe5","depends_on_id":"bd-2b0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:55:11.563746228Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pe5","depends_on_id":"bd-ohsq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:55.957608250Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":59,"issue_id":"bd-2pe5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## README section content

Include:
- One-liner: what the demo is
- Best command: `cargo run --bin demo_showcase --features full,tracing`
- CI-safe command: `cargo run --bin demo_showcase --features full,tracing -- --no-live --no-screen --no-interactive` (or equivalent)
- Short bullet list of what it demonstrates
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:47:05Z"}]}
@@ -159,7 +159,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3m5v","title":"demo_showcase: end-to-end showcase binary (turned up to 11)","description":"Standalone `demo_showcase` binary that showcases rich_rust end-to-end in a cohesive, polished, real-world narrative (all features, turned up to 11).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:20.204349495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:03.129344912Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase"],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-3m5v","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Vision

Create a **standalone Rust binary** (`cargo run --bin demo_showcase`) that feels like a real production CLI app, but uses rich_rust to an almost ridiculous degree: polished, stylish, visually dense, and clearly useful.

The goal is not a scattered “feature zoo”. It should read like a cohesive product demo (think: deploy pipeline / ops dashboard / release assistant), with a narrative flow, sensible UI hierarchy, and a few “wow” moments.

## Why this matters

- Proves feature completeness (tables/panels/layout/live/logging/export/etc.) by exercising *everything together* under realistic constraints.
- Provides a high-signal artifact for future refactors (human eyeballs notice regressions immediately).
- Makes the library compelling to adopt: it demonstrates *usefulness*, not just correctness.

## High-level demo flow (expected experience)

1. **Hero screen**: branded title, palette/theme preview, environment capability panel (TTY, color system, width/height).
2. **Dashboard** (Layout): split-screen UI with header + sidebar + main pane.
3. **Live run**: progress bars/spinners + streaming logs; deliberate use of markup, emojis, hyperlinks, and named theme styles.
4. **Deep dives**: Markdown (release notes), Syntax (config/code), JSON (request/response), Pretty/Inspect (structs), Traceback (synthetic error path).
5. **Export**: optional HTML/SVG export bundle (captures output and writes files) with clear paths + instructions.
6. **Wrap-up**: summary table/tree + next steps.

## Non-negotiable constraints

- **Must not hang** in non-interactive contexts (CI / piped output).
- Live/alt-screen/interactive prompts must auto-disable when not a TTY (or via flags).
- Default run should be reasonably quick (configurable via `--speed` / `--quick`).
- Must compile and run cleanly with `--features full,tracing` (and degrade gracefully when features are missing).
- No `unsafe` code anywhere.

## Acceptance criteria (Epic-level)

- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase --features full,tracing` produces a cohesive, impressive end-to-end demo that clearly exercises: markup/styles/themes, tables, panels, rules, trees, columns/align/padding, layout engine, live updates, progress/spinners, logging (+ tracing if enabled), pretty/inspect, traceback, emoji replacement, hyperlinks, and HTML/SVG export.
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase` (no features) still runs without crashing; optional scenes display friendly “compile with …” guidance.
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase | cat` completes without interactive waits and without runaway ANSI control spam.

## Implementation notes

- Keep the binary self-contained: no network, no external assets required.
- Prefer a small hand-rolled CLI parser (avoid heavy deps).
- Treat this as a *product demo*: strong defaults + flags for power users (`--no-live`, `--screen`, `--export-dir`, etc.).
- Use `Theme` and named styles heavily so the output demonstrates the theming system in a way users can copy.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3p3h","title":"demo_showcase tests: export bundle e2e","description":"Add e2e tests for --export/--export-dir: verify files exist, are non-empty, and contain expected markers.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:12.267899509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:58.345192238Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","export","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3p3h","depends_on_id":"bd-11zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:55.593766613Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3p3h","depends_on_id":"bd-2j06","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:02.715888129Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3p3h","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:12.283280618Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3p3h","depends_on_id":"bd-3s5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:58.345156882Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3p3h","depends_on_id":"bd-86gp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:57.982529951Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3p3h","depends_on_id":"bd-gfe5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:00.311081740Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":70,"issue_id":"bd-3p3h","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Export test plan

Run e2e with a temp directory:
- `demo_showcase --export-dir <temp> --no-live --no-screen --no-interactive --quick --width 80 --height 24`

Verify:
- expected files exist (`.html`, `.svg`, optional `.css`)
- non-empty
- contain obvious markers (e.g. the demo title string)

## Acceptance criteria

- Export regressions are caught automatically.
- Logs include paths written for easy debugging.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:11:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3pwg","title":"demo_showcase: Markdown deep-dive scene","description":"Implement the Markdown scene using the Markdown renderable (release notes/runbook) with pager integration when interactive.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:09.788681887Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:26.299808318Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","markdown"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pwg","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:21.587916900Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pwg","depends_on_id":"bd-3iws","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:26.299779735Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pwg","depends_on_id":"bd-3vf2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:09.801352546Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pwg","depends_on_id":"bd-3w3u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:24.010676029Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":42,"issue_id":"bd-3pwg","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Content

Use a realistic markdown doc, e.g. “Release Notes + Runbook”:
- headings
- bullet lists
- code fences (which will also visually tie to Syntax scene)
- inline emphasis

## UX

- If interactive and `Pager` is enabled, show the markdown in a pager (best UX).
- Otherwise render directly to console.

## Feature gating

If `markdown` feature is disabled, show a styled panel with enable instructions.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:21Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3rhg","title":"demo_showcase: interactive helpers integration","description":"Use Status/Prompt/Pager to make the demo feel like a real CLI wizard when running in a TTY.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:55.668932689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:56.917382989Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","interactive"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rhg","depends_on_id":"bd-12nv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:56.917352933Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rhg","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:54.500826925Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rhg","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:55.684260804Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":15,"issue_id":"bd-3rhg","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

Make the showcase feel like a real operator tool, not just output. Use the new interactive helpers *only when safe* (TTY):

- **Prompt**: ask for scenario / speed / whether to export
- **Status**: spinner while “preparing deployment plan” / “running checks”
- **Pager**: show long Markdown (runbook/release notes) without dumping everything into scrollback

## Safety

- If `Console::is_terminal()` is false, Prompt/Pager must not block.
- Provide `--no-interactive` to disable even in a TTY (useful for scripts).

## Acceptance criteria

- Interactive mode improves UX but is never required to run the demo.
- `--no-interactive` yields deterministic non-blocking output.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3s5n","title":"demo_showcase: CLI completeness pass (seed + links)","description":"Complete the CLI contract: add missing flags (--seed, --links/--no-links, etc.) and align --help output with the finalized spec.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:11:46.788764478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:33:19.158199409Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T23:33:19.158139518Z","close_reason":"Completed: add --seed + --links flags (help/tests/README)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3s5n","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:11:46.801784165Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":73,"issue_id":"bd-3s5n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why this exists

Early scaffolding landed a basic parser, but the *final* demo experience needs a few additional flags to support determinism + UX parity with the spec.

## Required additions

- `--seed <u64>`
  - used to seed any pseudo-random demo data (service IDs, jitter, stage durations)
  - default should be stable (e.g. 0 or fixed constant) so `cargo test` snapshots don't drift

- `--links` / `--no-links`
  - control OSC8 hyperlink emission independent of other styling

- Ensure `--help` and `--list-scenes` reflect the actual supported flags and scenes.

## Acceptance criteria

- `--seed` parses and rejects invalid values (non-integer, negative, etc.).
- Deterministic outputs are stable across runs when the same seed/width/height are used.
- Hyperlink control is explicit and defaults to a sensible value (likely auto-enabled when interactive).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:11:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3svf","title":"Full rendering pipeline integration tests","description":"## Problem
We need integration tests that verify the ENTIRE rendering pipeline from user API call to final output string. This would have caught the Cell markup bug.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Console.print() with various renderables containing markup
- [ ] Capture actual output string and verify markup is processed
- [ ] Test Table -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test Panel -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test nested renderables (Table in Panel, etc.) with markup

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Check that no raw markup tags like [bold] remain in output
- Verify ANSI escape codes are present for styled content
- These are TRUE integration tests - testing the full stack

## Files to modify
- Create new tests/integration/*.rs if needed
- Or add to tests/conformance/ with clear naming","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:07.703397468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166416332Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166109694Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 5 full-pipeline integration tests to e2e_rendering.rs. Tests cover Table/Panel with markup, Cell::new vs from_markup differences, Console.print with markup, and nested markup styles. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
